element_introduction_order.mastery_context: core traversal; dual-vine holding enables speed boost
avoidable via jumping or vine-switching; stationary threat
tactical tool for area denial; clears all enemies touched
encourages speed; death penalty if timer depletes
can drop eggs as projectiles; more aggressive than Snapjaws
vertical movement variant; functionally similar to vines
roam wiring; requires timing to avoid; cannot be destroyed by fruit
goal trigger; reaching cage key triggers stage completion
must push 6 keys into locks on top platform; no key collection, spatial puzzle instead
environmental obstacle; fall damage also present

typical_relief_triggers: reaching a key and triggering stage exit (Mario flees)
successful fruit knockdown eliminating nearby threats
switching vines to dodge incoming enemies
typical_tension_triggers: bonus timer counting down near zero
multiple enemies converging (Nitpickers dropping eggs + Snapjaws)
high-platform traversal requiring precise vine timing
Stage 4 multi-key puzzle under time pressure
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: escalating difficulty with each loop through 4-stage cycle; tension maintained via timer pressure throughout; relief points at stage exits; difficulty increases on subsequent cycles through same stages

difficulty_curve.early_game: Stage 1 introduces all core mechanics (climbing, jumping, dodging, fruit knockdown) with forgiving Snapjaw placement; bonus timer establishes pacing pressure
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Stage 2-3 escalates with Nitpickers (aggressive projectile enemies) and new climbing surfaces (chains); Stage 3 hideout introduces Sparks (unkillable electrical hazards); multiple threat types compound
difficulty_curve.late_game: Stage 4 shifts to puzzle-solving (6-key locks) instead of key collection; difficulty ramps through repeated cycles where same 4 stages replay with increased enemy aggression; kill screen at level 22 due to integer overflow
notable_spikes: Stage 2 introduction of Nitpickers with egg projectiles
Stage 3 introduction of Sparks (cannot be destroyed)
Stage 4 transition to multi-key puzzle mechanic
Loop 2+ where initial 4 stages replay at higher difficulty

notable_level_patterns.description: Player navigates vertical platforming space filled with enemies, climbs toward caged Donkey Kong at top, collects key next to cage to trigger stage exit. Fruit knockdown mechanic provides tactical relief. Bonus timer creates constant low-level pressure.

Abrupt shift from key-collection to environmental puzzle: player must push 6 keys into corresponding locks on topmost platform. Requires understanding spatial layout and multi-step traversal. Sparks add danger without defeating mechanic.

In US version, stage order varies per cycle: 1-4-1-2-4-1-3-4-1-2-3-4, then repeats 1-2-3-4. Each subsequent cycle features same stages but increased enemy aggression. Creates escalating challenge without new environments.
